name: CardStack
description: Adaptive layout component that displays children in a grid, timeline, or carousel based on item count and variant.
details: Use for displaying collections of cards. Automatically switches between grid (1-4 items) and carousel (5+ items). Special timeline variant creates a zigzag layout for 3-6 items; automatically switches to carousel for 7+ items. Carousel offers auto-scroll or button controls (recommended: keep default buttons). Optional header supports centered or split textbox layout (split: tag + title + description left, buttons right; split-actions: tag + title left, buttons only right; split-description: tag + title left, description only right).

propsSchema.animationType: 'none' | 'opacity' | 'slide-up' | 'scale-rotate' | 'blur-reveal' | 'depth-3d' (required - controls GSAP scroll animations with stagger effect)
propsSchema.titleSegments?: Array<{ type: 'text', content: string } | { type: 'image', src: string, alt?: string }> - For inline images in title
propsSchema.buttons?: Array<{text: string, onClick?: () => void, href?: string}>
propsSchema.textboxLayout: 'default' | 'split' | 'split-actions' | 'split-description' | 'inline-image' (required - 'inline-image' uses titleSegments for rich text with images)
propsSchema.titleImageWrapperClassName?: string - For styling image wrapper in inline-image layout
propsSchema.titleImageClassName?: string - For styling images in inline-image layout

usageExample: <CardStack title="Features" buttons={[{ text: 'View All', href: 'features' }]} animationType="slide-up" textboxLayout="default">{/* Card components */}</CardStack>
